properties.text:
Smart Batch Resize — how it works

The node ALWAYS writes resized images to disk — connecting the outputs is optional.

Outputs:
📷 images → batch tensor, feed into PreviewImage or any IMAGE input
🔢 count  → number of images actually processed

Key settings:
📁 Input folder — pick with Browse folder, or paste a path
📐 Longest side — target size, aspect ratio is always preserved
🔢 Round to multiple of — snap dimensions (use 8 for SD/Flux)
🏷️ Filename prefix / Keep original filename / Add counter — build the output filename
📂 Create resolution subfolder / Custom output subfolder — where resized files land (default: input_folder/resized/)
⏭️ Skip existing files — don't reprocess files already in the output folder

▶️ Run Batch = same as the regular Run button — it just runs this node. No separate output folder needed: results are saved inside the input folder itself (in the resized/ subfolder).

Tip: leave the prefix empty and 'Keep original filename' on to preserve names, or turn it off and use a prefix + counter for a clean renumbered batch.
